message:
fix(abi/stdio): reserve final NUL slot when fmemopen write fills exact truncate-mode capacity

The prior fix (5fa742cd) taught `sync_fmemopen_full` to write a
terminating NUL *after* the content, but only when there was room
left in the caller's buffer. For `fmemopen(buf, n, "w")` and
`"w+"` where the caller writes exactly n bytes, there was no
trailing byte left to NUL-terminate, so the flushed buffer lacked
the terminator that glibc guarantees for truncating modes.

This has two halves:

1. abi/stdio_abi.rs: `sync_fmemopen_full` now detects "truncate
   mode AND wrote exactly capacity" and copies `size - 1` bytes
   instead of `size`, making room for the NUL it places at the
   `copy_len` slot. Read-only (`r+`) streams still copy the full
   `size` bytes and do not reserve a NUL slot.

2. core/stdio/file.rs: the in-memory backing path for truncate
   streams mirrors the ABI behaviour — after `backing.write` when
   the write landed exactly at the fixed-capacity boundary, the
   final slot is zeroed so readback under `w+` sees the terminator
   instead of the last data byte. Exposed `is_truncating()` so the
   ABI layer can ask without duplicating flag logic.

Tests (tests/stdio_abi_test.rs)
-------------------------------
Three new tests, each with an 8-byte buffer and an 8-byte payload:

  - `fmemopen_w_truncate_exact_capacity_preserves_final_nul_slot`
    opens `"w"`, writes 8 bytes, flushes. Expects "0123456\0"
    (last slot NUL), not "01234567".

  - `fmemopen_wplus_exact_capacity_readback_sees_terminal_nul`
    opens `"w+"`, writes 8, flushes, rewinds, reads 8 back.
    Expects readback to observe the reserved terminator.

  - `fmemopen_rplus_exact_capacity_does_not_reserve_final_nul_slot`
    opens `"r+"` (non-truncating), writes 8, flushes. Expects full
    "01234567" with no reserved NUL slot, proving the new code
    path is guarded on the truncation bit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
